content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach maintains a generally calm, non-agenda-laden presence for most of the conversation — questions are spare, single, and held with space (turns 2, 4, 6, 8, 10). There's no evangelizing, no diagnosing, no validation noise, and the coach doesn't argue the fearlessness frame. That's real VIEW. However, wonder is more procedural than alive: the questions feel like a careful sequence designed to get underneath the 'fine,' rather than emerging from genuine curiosity about what's actually present. The coach never names their own state, never offers a tentative read with 'maybe I'm off,' never lets vulnerability show. Most tellingly, at turn 14 — the exact moment the client arrives at the threshold the breakthrough requires ('Huh. That's a strange way to put it' — the texture of 'fine' just became noticeable) — the coach pivots away from the felt texture into a hypothetical/cognitive frame ('What would it mean if it were more than fine?'). That move betrays a subtle agenda to get somewhere other than where the client just arrived, and it's the kind of step-past that signals the coach is managing the arc rather than meeting the phenomenon. Empathy and wonder partly present; impartiality wobbles at the end; vulnerability absent.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Clean opening question, single, no agenda visible yet."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Spare, curious follow-up. Not stacked. Lets client lead."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Nicely calibrated — separates 'sitting down with' from 'launching.' Curious about the actual phenomenon."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects one phrase, then a single open invitation. Held space, no rush."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the micro-shift without interpreting it. Stays close to the client's experience."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'What's underneath fine?' — competent but slightly formulaic; treats fine as a cover rather than as a texture to be felt."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Pivots to hypothetical ('what would it mean if') exactly when client is on the edge of feeling the quality of 'fine.' Reveals an agenda to move past the numbness layer rather than stay with it."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "lets-go-there", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer"],
  "notes": "The conversation is on a reasonable track and the coach's stance is mostly clean, but turn 14 is the tell — at the moment 'fine' became a felt curiosity for the client, the coach moved to abstraction. That's the agenda leaking through and is what keeps this from a 3."
}
